Linux | DevOps | AWS | Azure | System Administration | Shell | Production Support | Interview Preparation
Linux is one of the most fundamental skills expected from DevOps Engineers, Cloud Engineers, Site Reliability Engineers (SREs), System Administrators, and Backend Developers. Whether you’re working with Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, CI/CD pipelines, or web servers, chances are your applications are running on Linux.
As a result, Linux questions appear in almost every technical interview. While many questions seem basic, interviewers often use them to evaluate your understanding of how Linux systems operate in real production environments.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Mapbox on UnsplashRecently, I compiled a list of some of the most commonly asked Linux interview questions. These questions cover system administration, service management, monitoring, automation, and troubleshooting — skills that every engineer should be comfortable with.
If you’re preparing for DevOps, Cloud, Linux Administration, or Infrastructure roles, the following questions are an excellent starting point.
1. What Is the Root User in Linux?
